methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $150M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $500M.

private.name: Cemvita
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $120M

data.narrative:
Rondo Energy is a Series B climate and energy company founded by John O'Donnell and Eric Trusiewicz that has raised $150M+. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of five same-stage, same-sector private companies—Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, CarbonCapture Inc., and Cemvita—spanning valuations from $120M to $500M, supplemented by one public comparable.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $150M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $285M. Peer-median analysis across the five climate and energy comparables produces a median valuation of $310M, with a range of $120M to $500M. The peer set exhibits meaningful dispersion, with only Reverion and CarbonCapture Inc. clustered near the median at $310M and $300M respectively.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated valuation range of $242M to $356M, with a median point of $298M. This range reflects the tension between the more conservative stage-progression result and the higher peer median, which may reflect stronger capital availability or differentiated positioning within the climate tech subset. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory relative to peer benchmarks, technology differentiation within the crowded decarbonization sector, and the representativeness of the peer set given wide valuation dispersion.
